behavior_summary: U8P_CEMHAT::hatch is a world/process-level spawner that creates and places items (and occasionally NPCs) according to current quest/encounter flags.

It selects one of several mutually-exclusive branches based on global flags (e.g., toldDagger, haveDagger, lithosMet, lothInterred) and performs creation, placement, optional teleportation, and completion flag writes so the branch does not repeat.

Some branches call a bulk helper (FREE::createItemsAtLocation) to generate many items, filter them by coordinates/height and isNpc checks, push accepted items into a list, teleport NPCs into acceptable positions, and set branch-done flags.

The haveDagger branch resets references, sets ceremonySoon, teleports/positions an NPC for a ceremony, invokes spawn/METHOD::func15E7 on that object and yields the process (suspend) to allow the spawned behavior to proceed.
notable_control_flow: Branch selection is driven by global flags: check toldDagger && !toldDaggerDone, haveDagger (then set ceremonySoon), lithosMet && !lithosMetDone, and lothInterred.
Each one-time branch sets a corresponding Done flag (e.g., toldDaggerDone, lithosMetDone) to guard against re-running.
Creation loop pattern: Item::create -> test/validate (z/x/y/isNpc) -> either Item::pop (place) or Item::destroy (cleanup) or Npc::teleport + push into a list returned by FREE::createItemsAtLocation.
Asynchronous coordination: spawn + METHOD::func15E7 is called and the process yields via suspend, implying multi-step or timed behaviors after teleport/spawn.
open_questions: Exact item type and subtype ids (numeric constants seen in calls) are present in the raw but their semantic meanings (which in-game item each id maps to) remain unknown from this script alone.

Whether local7/local6/local5 are persistent global flags or purely temporary encoded location descriptors used only in this process (metadata suggests they behave as temporaries but they were listed under written globals).

Precise timing/frequency and trigger for this process execution (process type 0x0210 indicates a world/area process but whether it is periodic, on area load, or triggered by another script is not explicit).
The exact semantics of METHOD::func15E7 invoked after spawn (what it does to the spawned object and how the suspend interacts with it) are not fully recoverable here.

main_function.name: U8P_CEMHAT::hatch
main_function.purpose: Spawn and place quest- or event-related items/NPCs in the world based on quest flags; prepare and signal a ceremony branch when appropriate; mark branches complete to avoid repetition.
entry_conditions: Invoked as a process (process type 0x0210) for the associated world/area or item instance rather than as a direct player dialogue handler.
Runtime branch entry depends on global state flags such as toldDagger, haveDagger, lithosMet, and lothInterred (and their corresponding Done flags).

function_map.name: U8P_CEMHAT::hatch
function_map.role: World/area utility process that spawns and positions items/NPCs and advances related quest flags.

external_calls.purpose: Allocate/create an item or NPC instance and return a handle for subsequent placement/inspection.
Place/spawn a created item at specific map coordinates or into a placement list/container.
Discard unwanted or invalid created items as cleanup when validation checks fail.
Add accepted created item handles into a list/collection returned by FREE::createItemsAtLocation.
Read an item's X coordinate to test whether it lies inside a target region.
Read an item's Y coordinate for region tests and placement validation.
Read an item's Z (height) and filter out items above/below thresholds (e.g., z>8 checks).
Determine whether a created item is an NPC so it can be teleported instead of treated as a static item.
Teleport NPC instances to target map/layer/coordinates for ceremony or randomized placements.
Bulk-create items in an area and return a collection/list handle for filtering and final placement.
Produce random integers used to randomize NPC teleport destinations or placement offsets.
Clear world/object reference slots before initiating a ceremony or teleport sequence.
Spawn or instantiate an object and/or call METHOD::func15E7 to initiate scripted behavior on it.
Invoke a method on the spawned object to drive multi-step behavior; the process then suspends to wait for it.
Yield the process (wait) after starting an asynchronous spawn/method sequence or to end the current execution slice.

reads.name: toldDagger
reads.likely_effect: Controls whether the 'told about dagger' spawn branch should run.

reads.name: toldDaggerDone
reads.likely_effect: Prevents re-running the toldDagger branch when set.

reads.name: haveDagger
reads.likely_effect: Triggers the ceremony branch which teleports an NPC and starts the spawn/method sequence.

reads.name: lithosMet
reads.likely_effect: Enables a bulk-spawn branch that creates many Lithos-related items for placement.

reads.name: lithosMetDone
reads.likely_effect: Guards against repeating the lithosMet branch once it has been executed.

reads.name: lothInterred
reads.likely_effect: Allows the Loth-interred item sequence to run (creates a sequence of items).

writes.name: toldDaggerDone
writes.likely_effect: Mark the toldDagger branch complete so it does not run again.

writes.name: ceremonySoon
writes.likely_effect: Signal that a ceremony sequence will occur soon (set by haveDagger branch).

writes.name: lithosMetDone
writes.likely_effect: Mark that the lithos-related spawning/placement has been performed.

writes.name: local7
writes.likely_effect: Appears to hold an encoded X coordinate or location descriptor used as an argument to FREE::createItemsAtLocation; likely a temporary in-process value rather than a persistent global.

writes.name: local6
writes.likely_effect: Appears to hold an encoded Y coordinate or location descriptor used for bulk-creation arguments; likely temporary.

writes.name: local5
writes.likely_effect: Small integer temporary used as a helper/coordinate value in filtering logic; likely non-persistent.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_CEMHAT::hatch is a world/process-level spawner that creates and places items (and occasionally NPCs) according to current quest/encounter flags.
